mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce equity discount rates and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market recovery and rising consumer income, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows zero predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, with a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods when INDPRO increases month-over-month, or the signal loses statistical significance during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above potential output signals strong aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Large monthly payroll additions indicate robust labor demand and rising household spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered months, or the signal shows no statistical edge with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the inflation signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment signals improving labor productivity and supply-side efficiency, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.
